claim_short: 1812.10669 Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces. Problem 4.15 asks whether X* is fully Mackey complete whenever X is weak-star sequentially dense in X**. Partial positive theorem / structural criterion: if X contains no ell_1 and admits a shrinking bidual-capturing separable projectional skeleton (bounded separable projection ranges P_sX, countable suprema with norm convergence of induced dual...
notes: registry_status=partial | review_focus=The main point to check is the abstract localization lemma, especially the use of the shrinking condition to pass from norming estimates on \(P_{s_n}X\) to the countable supremum range \(P_sX\). This is also the obstruction exposed for a full solution of...
packet_title: Partial solution packet: a shrinking-skeleton positive criterion for Problem 4.15
source_authors: A. J. Guirao, G. Martínez-Cervantes, J. Rodríguez
source_question: Source: A. J. Guirao, G. Martinez-Cervantes, and J. Rodriguez, "Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces", arXiv:1812.10669 / J. Math. Anal. Appl. 478 (2019), 776--789.
source_title: Completeness in the Mackey topology by norming subspaces
